# Terraform policy CLI

The Terraform policy CLI (`tfpolicy`) provides commands for validating and testing your policies locally before enforcing them in HCP Terraform.

@include "beta.mdx"

## Usage

```shell-session
$ tfpolicy [global options] <command> [command options] [arguments...]
```

## Global flags

The following flags are available for all `tfpolicy` commands:

- `--help`, `-h` - Show help information for the command
- `--version`, `-v` - Display the version of the `tfpolicy` binary

## Available commands

The `tfpolicy` CLI supports the following commands.

### `test`

Run policy tests to verify your policies work as expected.

```shell-session
$ tfpolicy test --policies=/path/to/policies
```

Refer to [`tfpolicy test` command reference](/terraform/policy/reference/cli/test) for detailed information.

### `validate`

Validate the syntax of your policy files without running tests.

```shell-session
$ tfpolicy validate --policies=/path/to/policies
```

Refer to [`tfpolicy validate` command reference](/terraform/policy/reference/cli/validate) for detailed information.

### `version`

Display version information for the tfpolicy CLI.

```shell-session
$ tfpolicy version
```

Refer to [`tfpolicy version` command reference](/terraform/policy/reference/cli/version) for detailed information.

## Install

To install the Terraform policy CLI, refer to [Install the Terraform policy CLI](/terraform/policy/install).
